alertitem.alert: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
alertitem.name: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
alertitem.desc: Content Security Policy (CSP) is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks.
alertitem.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

alertitem.name: Strict-Transport-Security Header Not Set
alertitem.desc: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) is a web security policy mechanism whereby a web server declares that complying user agents should only interact with it using secure HTTPS connections.
alertitem.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to enforce Strict-Transport-Security.

alertitem.name: Cross-Domain Misconfiguration
alertitem.desc: Web browser data loading may be possible due to a Cross Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) misconfiguration on the web server. The Access-Control-Allow-Origin header is set to *, which means any domain can make cross-origin requests.
alertitem.solution: Ensure that sensitive data is not available in an unauthenticated manner. Configure the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header to a more restrictive set of domains.

alertitem.name: Permissions Policy Header Not Set
alertitem.desc: The Permissions-Policy header is not set. The deprecated Feature-Policy header was found (payment 'self') but should be migrated to the newer Permissions-Policy format.
alertitem.solution: Set the Permissions-Policy header instead of the deprecated Feature-Policy header.

alertitem.name: Secure Pages Include Mixed Content
alertitem.desc: The page served over HTTP rather than HTTPS. All web traffic should use HTTPS to ensure data confidentiality and integrity.
alertitem.solution: Serve the application over HTTPS and redirect HTTP to HTTPS.

alertitem.name: Modern Web Application
alertitem.desc: The application appears to be a modern web application. Automatic testing may not find all vulnerabilities.
alertitem.solution: This is an informational finding — manual testing is recommended.

alertitem.name: Re-examine Cache-control Directives
alertitem.desc: The cache-control header is set to "public, max-age=0". While max-age=0 forces revalidation, sensitive responses should use "no-store" or "no-cache" directives to prevent caching.
alertitem.solution: Use Cache-Control: no-store for responses containing sensitive information.

alertitem.name: Non-Storable Content
alertitem.desc: The response contains an ETag header which can be used for tracking users across sessions.
alertitem.solution: Remove ETag headers if not needed for caching, or ensure they do not leak sensitive information.

alertitem.name: Information Disclosure - Debug Error Messages
alertitem.desc: The X-Recruiting header discloses an internal path: /#/jobs. This is specific to OWASP Juice Shop but represents the broader pattern of information disclosure through custom headers.
alertitem.solution: Remove custom debug or recruiting headers from production deployments.
